prompt: Develop a CampusLearn Operations Suite system for managing an institution’s offline learning catalog, shared facilities, and compliance risk signals in one consistent portal. The Dioxus web UI runs as a responsive admin-and-user console where Staff Authors create structured courses with sections and lessons, attach rich text plus locally uploaded media (PDF/MP4/PNG under 500 MB each), apply tags, and manage draft-to-release versions that clearly show what changed since the last approved release. Department Reviewers see an approval queue that requires a two-step signoff for any publish or unpublish action, with a required release note and an effective date/time (MM/DD/YYYY, 12-hour time) so a catalog update can be scheduled for the next term. Faculty and Students browse only published versions, with clear “Updated on” indicators and read-only access to prior versions for 180 days. The same UI includes Shared Resource Booking for meeting rooms, the clubhouse, and parking permits: users select a resource, pick a time slot, and receive immediate conflict feedback; bookings can be requested up to 90 days ahead, limited to 2 active reservations per user per resource, and capped at 4 hours per single meeting-room reservation. Rescheduling is allowed up to 2 times; cancellations within 2 hours of start create a breach record that is visible to approvers and can trigger temporary booking restrictions after 3 breaches in 60 days. In-app alerts appear in a notification center when approvals are pending, when a booking is approved/denied, and when risk rules flag anomalous activity.The backend uses Rocket to expose a REST-style API consumed by the Dioxus frontend, with MySQL persisting course hierarchies, content versions, media metadata, bookings, approvals, breaches, risk rules, and audit trails. All functionality must run without internet access and without external dependencies: authentication uses local username/password only, passwords require at least 12 characters with complexity rules, sessions are JWT-based for stateless API calls, and administrative actions require re-auth within 15 minutes. RBAC enforces menu/button/data-scope permissions so an Author cannot self-approve, and data access is restricted by department and term. Concurrency-safe booking uses database transactions and unique time-range conflict checks to prevent double-booking under parallel requests, while rule validation enforces resource hours (for example, 7:00 AM–10:00 PM) and maintenance blackouts. The anomaly and risk engine runs on a scheduled job (default every 15 minutes) to detect abnormal compensation entries for adjunct assignments, suspected duplicate internship/job postings, blacklisted employers, and unusual posting frequency (for example, more than 20 postings per employer in 24 hours), with configurable thresholds and in-app subscriptions; optional webhooks are supported only to on-prem endpoints and are queued locally when unreachable. Security requirements include HTTPS for all connections, signed API requests for system integrations with anti-replay nonces expiring in 5 minutes, strict input validation plus SQL injection/XSS/CSRF defenses, rate limiting (for example, 120 requests/minute per account) and basic anti-scraping, field-level masking for SSNs and bank details, encryption at rest for sensitive columns, immutable operation audit logs retained for 7 years, security-event tracking, and offline personal-data export/deletion workflows with administrator approval.
